message:
refactor(frankenterm-core): centralize storage proptest async runtime on shared helper (ft-iq339 / ft-3tvvt)

Follow-on from ft-3tvvt, continuing the centralization started in
ft-upvjr (113a6668e). Each of the three proptest cases in
`storage::proptest_tests` was independently constructing
`RuntimeBuilder::multi_thread().build().expect(...).block_on(...)` per
case — three copies of the same boilerplate, no panic-catching, no
runtime-drop absorption.

New helper in `storage.rs`:

- `run_storage_proptest_async<F, T>(future: F) -> T` — multi-thread
  sibling of `run_storage_async_test`. Wraps `block_on` and the
  runtime drop / `clear_runtime_handle()` calls in `catch_unwind`
  envelopes (same shape as `run_storage_async_test`), but returns
  the future's value so the proptest can run its `prop_assert_*`
  checks *outside* the async block. The multi-thread runtime is
  preserved (the proptests exercise StorageHandle's writer + reader
  concurrency under proptest-driven load — current_thread isn't a
  drop-in here).

`storage::proptest_tests` rewires:

- `prop_seq_monotonic_per_pane`, `prop_fts_finds_inserted_text`,
  `prop_fts_respects_pane_scope` now each call
  `super::run_storage_proptest_async(async { … })` and bind the
  returned tuple/vec.
- Local `use crate::runtime_async::{CompatRuntime, RuntimeBuilder};`
  import dropped — no longer needed at the proptest layer.

Same TLS-destructor-panic guard semantics that motivated ft-3tvvt
now extend to the proptest cases: a panic during `runtime.block_on`,
runtime drop, or `clear_runtime_handle()` no longer leaks across
proptest case boundaries.

Verification: `cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib
--no-default-features` → clean (16.59s).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
